fields.brief_block: Further, “hindsight has no place in an assessment of the performance of trial counsel.” Clark v. State, 315 Ga. 1, 4 (2022).
fields.opinion_block: Trial counsel also testified that perhaps he should have asked the trial court to question the juror, but “hindsight has no place in an assessment of the performance of trial counsel.” Keener v. State, 301 Ga. 848, 850 (2) (804 SE2d 383) (2017) (cleaned up).
fields.finding_analysis: Clark v. State is itself an ineffective-assistance-of-counsel case, and it quotes this exact principle approvingly as part of its analysis affirming that trial counsel's investigation of an alleged juror-misconduct incident fell within reasonable performance, so the proposition is accurately grounded in the cited opinion. One caveat for the lawyer: the language originates in Keener v. State, 301 Ga. 848, 850 (2017), and Clark quotes it from Keener rather than coining it; the quote also appears near the end of the opinion (internal page 9) rather than at the cited pincite of page 4, so the pinpoint citation is off even though the substance is fully supported.
